description:
You are the on-call SRE for a small in-house Kubernetes Operator.  
  The dev team has saved the operator’s latest run-time log to  
  /home/user/k8s-operator/logs/operator.log  

  Your goal: isolate every “level=error” line (and only those lines) in the
  original order and save them to a brand-new file called  
  /home/user/k8s-operator/logs/errors_only.log  

  Requirements that the automated test will check:

  1. The output file path must be exactly  
     /home/user/k8s-operator/logs/errors_only.log  

  2. The file must contain **only** the lines that match this
     extended-regex pattern (without the surrounding / slashes):  
        ^20[0-9]{2}.*level=error.*$  
     • This means every line must start with a year-style timestamp (e.g. 2023-08-01T…)  
       and somewhere in the line contain “level=error”.  
     • No extra blank lines or trailing spaces are allowed.  
     • Preserve the original order found in operator.log.  

  3. After creating the file, display on the terminal the number of
     lines that ended up in errors_only.log (a simple integer on its own line
     is fine).  The test harness does not validate the console output, but it
     helps a human reviewer.  

  4. You are limited to a short series of basic shell commands
     (about 3-4 commands in total) to accomplish this task
     – e.g. moving into the directory, running grep/egrep, verifying with wc,
       etc.  Do not alter the original operator.log.

  When finished, the only new artefact on disk should be
  /home/user/k8s-operator/logs/errors_only.log that satisfies rule 2.
truth:
Pre-existing directory layout (already present when the agent starts):

  /home/user/k8s-operator/
      logs/
          operator.log    (permissions 0644, regular text file)

  Exact contents of /home/user/k8s-operator/logs/operator.log
  (each line ends with a single Unix newline “\n”):

    2023-08-01T08:30:12Z level=info    msg="Starting controller"
    2023-08-01T08:30:13Z level=error   code=CM001 ns=default name=app-config   msg="Missing ConfigMap"
    2023-08-01T08:30:14Z level=warning msg="Retry reconcile"
    2023-08-01T08:30:15Z level=error   code=SV001 ns=prod    name=web-svc      msg="Service not found"
    2023-08-01T08:30:16Z level=info    msg="Reconcile complete"
    2023-08-01T08:30:17Z level=error   code=DEP404 ns=staging name=api-deployment msg="Deployment failed"
    2023-08-01T08:31:18Z level=info    msg="Starting controller"
    2023-08-01T08:31:19Z level=error   code=CM001 ns=default name=app-config   msg="Missing ConfigMap"
    2023-08-01T08:31:20Z level=warning msg="Retry reconcile"
    2023-08-01T08:31:21Z level=error   code=SV001 ns=prod    name=web-svc      msg="Service not found"

  Lines expected to match the regex (and therefore must appear,
  in the shown order, in errors_only.log):

    2023-08-01T08:30:13Z level=error   code=CM001 ns=default name=app-config   msg="Missing ConfigMap"
    2023-08-01T08:30:15Z level=error   code=SV001 ns=prod    name=web-svc      msg="Service not found"
    2023-08-01T08:30:17Z level=error   code=DEP404 ns=staging name=api-deployment msg="Deployment failed"
    2023-08-01T08:31:19Z level=error   code=CM001 ns=default name=app-config   msg="Missing ConfigMap"
    2023-08-01T08:31:21Z level=error   code=SV001 ns=prod    name=web-svc      msg="Service not found"

  Therefore the correct /home/user/k8s-operator/logs/errors_only.log must consist
  of those 5 lines, in exactly that order, each terminated with “\n”.
name: task_000000_aa74e439
